canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, SLURP is consistently described as a spoken language understanding dataset. Its most common application appears to be as a measurement instrument for evaluating model capabilities, specifically for tasks like `Information extraction` and `Intention recognition`. One paper highlights its use for "slot filling from reference transcriptions," drawing on the dataset's annotations for "scenarios, actions and entities." The dataset is composed of "single-turn user interactions with a home assistant," containing both audio recordings and text transcriptions, though at least one study reports using only the text for training. A different application is also noted, where SLURP serves as a "seed corpus for generating dialogues." In this context, it is used to construct new corpora by leveraging its "diverse intents and interwoven mode switches."
papers: CARE: A Disagreement Detection Framework with Concept Alignment and Reasoning Enhancement

Improving Semantic Understanding in Speech Language Models via Brain-tuning
Joint Fine-tuning and Conversion of Pretrained Speech and Language Models towards Linear Complexity

definitions.text: SLURP is a spoken language understanding dataset used here for slot filling from reference transcriptions of single-turn user interactions.
definitions.paper: Bayesian WeakS-to-Strong from Text Classification to Generation

relationships.paper: SALMONN: Towards Generic Hearing Abilities for Large Language Models
relationships.evidence: SLURP dataset (Bastianelli et al., 2020) was used which contains 16.5k sentences and 72k audio recordings of single-turn user interactions with a home assistant, annotated with scenarios, actions and entities. (Section 5.1)
